title: Early colonization projects
description: Check your understanding of the early colonization projects in North America.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Why was England slower than Spain to establish colonies in the Americas?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Think about what crises inside England—religion, Ireland, and poverty—kept the country busy at home.

choices.content: Religious conflict, wars in Ireland, and economic depression kept England occupied at home.
England had little interest in overseas trade or competing with Spain in the Americas.
England lacked ships and sailors to make the long, dangerous journey across the Atlantic Ocean.
England already had colonies in Africa and Asia, so the Americas were not considered important.

assessment_items.question_content:
**How did conflict between Catholics and Protestants affect England’s ability to colonize in the 1500s?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Think about the back-and-forth between Protestant Elizabeth I and Catholic Mary, and how that disrupted expansion.

choices.content: It created instability that kept England focused on internal problems.
It united all English leaders and encouraged early colonial expansion.
It forced England to make an alliance with Spain to fund expeditions.
It pushed England to focus primarily on trade with Asia and Africa.

assessment_items.question_content:
**How did England’s experience in Ireland affect English colonists' encounters with Native Americans in North America?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Think about what English soldiers learned from fighting in Ireland and how those lessons shaped their approach to Native peoples in North America.

choices.content: They treated Native peoples as enemies to be conquered and displaced.
They expected to convert Native peoples to Protestant Christianity peacefully.
They formed equal alliances with Native nations to expand English trade networks.
They avoided conflict with Native groups by keeping settlements far from Indigenous lands.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What was the role of joint stock companies in England’s colonization of North America?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Think about how early corporations worked—many people invested small amounts, so no one made or lost all their money on any one company.

choices.content: They spread the risks and rewards of colonies among many investors.
They supplied enslaved laborers to early colonies like Jamestown.
They guaranteed colonists lifelong rights to land in the North American colonies.
They replaced Parliament as England’s main decision-making body in the colonies.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What role did sailors like Sir Francis Drake play in England’s overseas ventures before Jamestown?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Think about how privateers like Drake allowed England to profit from Spain’s work without building colonies.

choices.content: They attacked Spanish ships and seized treasure for England.
They built permanent English colonies throughout the Caribbean.
They traded with Spain to obtain goods like silver and sugar.
They funded the creation of the Virginia Company with their profits.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What new financial tool helped England fund colonies like Jamestown in the early 1600s?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Think about the early version of corporations that allowed many to invest in risky colonial ventures.

choices.content: Joint Stock Companies that spread risk among investors
Royal monopolies that guaranteed profits for the Crown
Religious tithes collected by the Anglican Church
Loans from Spain that could be used to build settlements

assessment_items.question_content:
**Why did the Virginia Company fund Jamestown in 1607?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Think about why private investors funded colonization instead of the Crown.

choices.content: To earn profits for their investors from raw materials
To create a safe and permanent settlement for English Catholics
To build military bases and attack Spanish colonies in Mexico
To complete a scientific mission to map the Americas

assessment_items.question_content:
**What was one major reason that the Jamestown colony in Virginia was able to survive despite early difficulties?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Think about what economic development helped make Virginia sustainable after its early years of near failure.

choices.content: The introduction of tobacco cultivation provided an economic foundation for the colony.
The discovery of large gold and silver deposits attracted new waves of settlers.
The establishment of religious freedom convinced persecuted groups to immigrate.
The early use of enslaved African labor eliminated food shortages in the colony.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the main significance of the Roanoke colony in North America?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Think about what happened when John White returned to Roanoke after several years in England.

choices.content: The mysterious disappearance of its settlers led to ongoing debate and historical investigation.
The colony’s profitable tobacco exports made it England’s first successful permanent settlement.
Its settlers established peaceful and lasting alliances with local Indigenous groups.
The colony inspired more Puritans to migrate to the Americas in search of religious freedom.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Why is the English settlement at Roanoke remembered as the Lost Colony?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Think about the mystery of Roanoke and why no one can say for sure what happened.

choices.content: The colonists disappeared, leaving only the word "Croatoan" carved on a post.
The colonists fought a major battle with Spain and were defeated.
The colonists starved during their first winter, according to records that they left behind.
The colonists struggled at first but eventually moved to French fur-trading posts.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What caused early cooperation between the English settlers and the Secotan people at Roanoke to collapse?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Think about how a small argument over missing goods quickly turned into a violent attack that ended trust between the English and the Secotan.

choices.content: The English accused the Secotans of stealing and attacked them to seize food supplies.
The Secotans refused to trade food or help the English survive the winter.
The English believed the Spanish planned to invade the colony and turned on their Native allies in fear.
The English decided to leave Roanoke peacefully and settle with nearby Native nations.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Why did the English attempt to create a colony at Roanoke in the 1580s?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Think about how Roanoke fit into England’s rivalry with Spain.

choices.content: They wanted a base in the Americas to challenge Spanish power and search for wealth.
They hoped to establish a peaceful farming community with Native American allies.
They planned to focus on converting Native Americans to Protestantism.
They sought to create a safe haven for English Catholics who faced persecution.
